title: Eastern England: Harrington, Cambridge, Oxburgh Hall & Norfolk

main_route.excerpt: We left St. George's at 9.30. pm after a Malawi Concert & reached a campsite next to Harrington*** Secret Aviation Museum
reached a campsite next to Harrington*** Secret Aviation Museum (Brit & US planes taking people in & out of France, carpet bagging) It was dark when we arrived
We set off to see Rosie, Jane & Robert in Cambridge. They live in a nice modern house
After lunch in a field we drove on to Oxburgh Hall which belongs to an old Catholic family, the Bedingfields. There was a priest's hole which we managed to climb into
On to camp at Mileham north of Swaffham. ... Mileham was very pretty, enclosed in lots of trees. No loo or showers. Very green & quiet.
Our route was very zig-zag past tiny villages not even on the map to East Dereham. The church was beautiful.
He said he was off to do jazz at the pub courtyard at Reepham. We ended up going in the same direction & stopped to listen.
finally reached Blickling Hall, where we had a good lunch (chicken & asparagus) in the canteen. The Hall is beautiful, partly Jacobean. Anne Boleyn was born there.
We drove south to near Halesworth. The campsite owner is friendly & has sold me some eggs. Mindon House * * * * Blyford. loo in farm house laundry room, no shower, lovely big field

main_route.original_wording: Oundle on the R. Nene to Fairline Marina
main_route.excerpt: Then we set off for Oundle on the R. Nene to Fairline Marina where Chad is hoping to do lights.
We had a good pub lunch - Simon home-made beefburgers & Eve scampi & chips at Upper Benefield in a shady garden.
On to Corby where Chad hopes a factory will do extruded aluminium.
At tea time we passed the Buddhist Centre at near Naseby.
After this we drove on through the heat for home. At 5.30. we took the route along Bristol Rd. + rt. at Cannon Hill up Priory Rd.

boundary_flags.issue: boundary_mixed: opening paragraph belongs to the PREVIOUS trip (St. George's campsite, Malawi Concert, waving goodbye to William & Bryony in their van 'Helen'). This trip begins at 'We left St. George's at 9.30. pm'.
boundary_flags.excerpt: We waved goodbye to William & Bryony in their van, "Helen" & packed up in the drizzle & left. __June 17th Friday__ We left St. George's at 9.30. pm

summary: A short UK camping holiday through eastern England in June 2005. Departing from St George's, the family camped at Harrington (Northamptonshire), then toured Cambridge and a string of National Trust houses in Norfolk (Oxburgh Hall, Blickling Hall) with overnight stops at Mileham and near Halesworth, before driving home to Birmingham via Oundle, Corby and Naseby.
extraction_notes: GB-only trip; no sea/tunnel crossings (the 'France' mention on B_0056_L refers to WWII carpet-bagging flights from the Harrington aviation museum, not travel). Trip starts at an explicit non-home departure 'St. George's' (mixed_start: true; opening paragraph of B_0056_L is the tail of the previous trip). 'St. George's' could not be reliably geolocated from the bundle (no county given) — recorded as departure with confidence 0.6 / approximate. date_end is null: no closing date is recorded; the final day is dated only by narrative progression from the June 17th start. Travellers limited to Simon and Eve as confidently named on this trip's pages (first-person diary, likely Eve); 'Chad' (business contact for marine/factory lighting work) and the dog 'Tonic/Tonie/Toni' appear but are not listed as travellers. Cambridge, Oxburgh Hall, Blickling Hall and East Dereham are visited in linear progression between overnight bases (not same-base day trips), so there are no excursions.
